summary: The app has a clean minimal chat UI with basic error handling and loading state, but critical UX gaps exist: no query history (users lose previous Q&A), no example questions for first-time users, raw Python dict display as 'answer', no accessibility labels on interactive elements, and the results table lacks number formatting for baseball stats. The core query experience is a single-shot interaction with no context retention.

findings.title: No query history — users lose all previous questions and answers on page reload
findings.detail: Each query replaces the previous result entirely. Users cannot see their question history, compare answers, or reference earlier queries. Refreshing the page loses everything. For a stats exploration tool, context continuity is essential.
findings.recommendation: Add a questions[] state array that accumulates {question, response, error} entries. Render as a scrollable chat-style list. Persist to localStorage for cross-session retention. Use a custom useQueryHistory() hook.

findings.title: No example questions for new users — blank page with no guidance
findings.detail: A first-time user sees only an input box with a placeholder. They don't know what kinds of questions work, what data is available, or what format to use. This increases friction and reduces engagement.
findings.recommendation: Add 3-4 clickable example question chips below the input: 'Who leads the AL in home runs?', '2025 season OPS leaders', 'Shohei Ohtani career batting average'. On click, populate the input and auto-submit.

findings.title: Loading indicator is just '...' on the button — no visual feedback for long LLM calls
findings.detail: LLM + SQL execution can take 2-5 seconds. The only indicator is the button text changing from 'Ask' to '...'. There is no spinner, no skeleton loader, no progress message. Users may think the app is broken.
findings.recommendation: Add a visible loading skeleton or spinner in the results area when loading=true. Show a message like 'Analyzing your question...' then 'Running query...' for staged feedback. Use Tailwind animate-pulse on a skeleton card.

findings.title: Results table column headers show raw DB column names
findings.detail: Table headers display raw database column names like 'player_mlb_id', 'batting_avg', 'wrc_plus'. These are developer-facing identifiers, not human-readable labels. Baseball fans expect 'AVG', 'OPS', 'wRC+'.
findings.recommendation: Create a columnDisplayNames map: { batting_avg: 'AVG', home_runs: 'HR', plate_appearances: 'PA', wrc_plus: 'wRC+' }. Apply to table headers. For unmapped columns, convert snake_case to Title Case.

findings.title: No number formatting for baseball statistics
findings.detail: All values are rendered with String(val). Baseball averages should show 3 decimal places (.300, not 0.3), ERA should show 2 decimals, WAR 1 decimal, counting stats should have commas for thousands. Currently, values display as raw floats.
findings.recommendation: Create a formatStatValue(columnName, value) utility. Detect stat type from column name and format accordingly: batting_avg/obp/slg → .toFixed(3), era/whip → .toFixed(2), war → .toFixed(1), counting stats → toLocaleString().

findings.title: Textarea component missing aria-label for screen readers
findings.detail: The query Textarea has no associated <label> element or aria-label attribute. Screen readers will announce it generically as 'text area' without context about its purpose.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-label='Ask a baseball statistics question' to the Textarea. Or wrap with a visually-hidden <label> element using sr-only class.

findings.title: Results table missing scope and caption for accessibility
findings.detail: The results table has no <caption>, no scope='col' on <th> elements. Screen readers cannot properly associate data cells with their column headers.
findings.recommendation: Add <caption className='sr-only'>Query results</caption>, add scope='col' to all <th> elements, add scope='row' to first <td> in each row if it's an identifier column.

findings.title: No empty state when query returns zero results
findings.detail: When a query returns zero results, the Answer card shows 'No results found for your question.' but the Results card simply doesn't render (result.length > 0 check). This is ok but could be more helpful — suggest reformulating the question or show what data is available.
findings.recommendation: When result is empty, show a helpful empty state: 'No matching data found. Try asking about batting stats for a specific player or season. We currently have batting stats for qualified hitters.'

findings.title: No responsive table behavior for mobile
findings.detail: The results table has overflow-x-auto which enables horizontal scrolling, but there's no visual indicator that the table scrolls horizontally on mobile. Baseball stat tables can have 10+ columns making them very wide.
findings.recommendation: Add a subtle gradient fade on the right edge when table overflows. Or add a 'scroll for more' hint below the table. Consider a card-based layout on mobile breakpoints (sm:) instead of a table.

findings.title: Error messages may expose internal details to users
findings.detail: The backend returns 'SQL execution error: {e}' which may contain PostgreSQL error messages with table names, column names, or query details. These are passed directly to the frontend error display. Users see developer-facing error messages.
findings.recommendation: Backend: log the full error server-side, return a generic user-friendly message like 'We couldn't run that query. Try rephrasing your question.' Frontend: map known error codes to user-friendly messages.
